assertions.title: Document replacement chain

steps.action: Upload document V1, then upload V2 for same requirement
steps.expected: Old doc isCurrent=false, new doc has replacesDocumentId pointing to old
steps.observed: Old doc isCurrent=false, but new doc has replacesDocumentId=null

assertions.issues: BUG FOUND: replacesDocumentId is not being set when uploading a new version. The markPreviousDocumentsAsNotCurrent function does not capture and pass the previous document ID to the new document's replacesDocumentId field.

assertions.title: Only one isCurrent per requirement

steps.action: Upload multiple versions and query for isCurrent documents
steps.expected: Exactly 1 document with isCurrent=true per (requestId, requirementKey)

assertions.issues: HTTP API returned 500 error - tested via direct service call instead. Note: VAL-DOC-012 bug (replacesDocumentId not set) does not affect this assertion since isCurrent is correctly managed.

frictions.description: HTTP API endpoints for documents return 500 Internal Server Error for all /api/rpc/documents/* routes
frictions.resolution: Used direct service function calls via bun to test functionality instead

frictions.description: The uploadCitizenDocument function does not set replacesDocumentId when creating a new version
frictions.resolution: Bug confirmed in source code - markPreviousDocumentsAsNotCurrent() is called but the previous document ID is not captured and passed to the new document's replacesDocumentId field

blockers.description: HTTP API layer for documents endpoints is not functioning - all /api/rpc/documents/* routes return 500 Internal Server Error. This prevents testing via curl as specified in the task requirements.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried multiple curl formats including jsonrpc 2.0 wrapper, direct params, different headers. Session endpoints work correctly (session.get returns 200). The issue appears to be specific to the documents router's RPC handler.

summary: Tested 13 assertions (VAL-DOC-001 through VAL-DOC-013). 12 passed, 1 failed. The HTTP API for documents endpoints is broken (returns 500 for all /api/rpc/documents/* routes), so testing was performed using direct service function calls via bun. The functional tests confirm that document upload, listing, review actions, and status transitions work correctly. However, VAL-DOC-012 failed because the replacesDocumentId field is not being populated when uploading a new version of a document - this is a bug in the markPreviousDocumentsAsNotCurrent function which needs to capture and return the previous document ID.
